author: codex
scope: Codex-only relation endpoint-shape cleanup prep; deterministic inspection only; no durable relation inputs mutated

prior_prep_artifact_hits.note: Prior rule/data triage identified it as a data error, but no cleanup prep artifact retired it.
Prior cleanup batch030 explicitly deferred it because the source did not name a clean route target.
No prior cleanup prep artifact found for this submission.

current_shape.validator_result: unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape: serves_route expects project|corridor -> route, got project -> entity

source_evidence.short_quote: thanked the Metro-North workers who got service back up and running quickly
source_evidence.evidence_read: The cited public-comment block discusses Metro-North storm recovery, resiliency funding, congestion pricing, and fare increases. It does not mention Penn Station Access, Bronx stations, or a route endpoint.

existing_coverage.note: Existing valid route-scope coverage links Penn Station Access to the New Haven Line.
Existing valid agency-role coverage already associates Penn Station Access with Metro-North in better-supported sources.

prepared_changes.why_no_replacement: A serves_route replacement would require a route object not named in the cited block. A project -> entity replacement would duplicate existing valid Metro-North/PSA coverage and would still not be supported by this evidence block.

proposed_retired_json_entry.reason: Codex relation cleanup batch061 retires malformed serves_route project -> entity relation relation_meeting-doc-121091-psa-bronx-stations. serves_route requires a route object, but the submitted object is a Metro-North entity and the cited meeting_doc_121091#p002_c0004 public-comment block does not mention Penn Station Access, Bronx stations, or a route endpoint. Existing valid PSA route and Metro-North role coverage remains in other records.

current_shape.validator_result: unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape: operated_by expects project|route|corridor -> entity, got treatment_component -> entity

source_evidence.short_quote: Approval was requested to award a five year non-competitive estimated quantities contract
source_evidence.evidence_read: The block states that Metro-North procurement requested approval to award a contract to Powell Electric Systems. It supports the treatment/procurement record, but not a treatment_component operated_by Metro-North relation.

existing_coverage.display_name: New Haven Line Substation Circuit Breaker Replacement
existing_coverage.note: The accepted treatment component already preserves the source-backed New Haven Line substation circuit-breaker replacement scope, locations, and procurement nature.

prepared_changes.why_no_replacement: A validator-compatible operated_by replacement would need a project, route, or corridor subject. The source currently has only a treatment_component endpoint for this procurement, and creating a broader project endpoint is outside this bounded relation-only prep. A relation to Powell would also require a resolved Powell entity endpoint not present in the current canonical same-source endpoint set.

proposed_retired_json_entry.reason: Codex relation cleanup batch061 retires malformed operated_by treatment_component -> entity relation relation_procurement-metronorth-circuit-breakers. operated_by requires a project, route, or corridor subject, but the submitted subject is a treatment_component. The cited meeting_doc_102686#p012_c0012 block supports a Metro-North procurement contract fact already preserved on the treatment component, and it does not provide a high-confidence validator-compatible replacement relation.

current_shape.validator_result: unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape: operates_on_corridor expects route|entity -> corridor, got project -> entity

source_evidence.short_quote: The ABLE program began on a small scale with 123 buses across 8 routes
source_evidence.evidence_read: The block states ABLE/ACE rollout facts: initial 123 buses across 8 routes in Manhattan and Brooklyn, first M15 SBS launch in October 2019, 2023 legislative expansion, and growth to 58 routes.

existing_coverage.note: Same-source valid relation already links ACE to the ABLE Program Established event.
Same-source valid relation already links ACE to the M15 SBS ACE/ABLE launch event using the same p029_c0006 block.
Same-source valid relation covers the 2023 legislative expansion context from the same ACE section.
A broader ACE source already carries route-level coverage for many concrete ACE routes; this malformed row's borough/entity endpoint does not add a valid corridor fact.

prepared_changes.why_no_replacement: The cited phrase names routes and boroughs, not a corridor record. Same-source project -> event relations already preserve the establishment and launch semantics, so adding a looser replacement would duplicate valid coverage or invent a corridor endpoint.

proposed_retired_json_entry.reason: Codex relation cleanup batch061 retires malformed operates_on_corridor project -> entity relation relation_rel-ace-starts-at-manhattan-brooklyn. operates_on_corridor requires route|entity -> corridor, but the submitted endpoints are the ACE project and the MTA entity. The cited meeting_doc_201621#p029_c0006 block supports ACE/ABLE rollout facts already covered by valid same-source has_timeline_event relations, not a project-to-entity corridor relation.

expected_delta.caveat: Assumes no concurrent relation endpoint-shape work lands before conductor applies relation061.

apply_recommendation.recommended_audit_reason: Retire three remaining malformed endpoint-shape relations without replacement because source-backed facts are either unsupported by the cited block or already covered by valid canonical records.

commands_run_read_only: pwd && rg -n selected relation ids in data/canonical/relations.jsonl data/submissions data/submission-overrides/retired.json data/review_notes
sed reads of prior relation cleanup and rule-or-data prep artifacts
rg searches for selected submission ids and record ids in data/review_notes, data/submissions, and retired.json
sqlite3 read-only endpoint-kind joins for selected relation ids
sqlite3 read-only same-source endpoint and existing-coverage checks
jq targeted source block reads for meeting_doc_121091, meeting_doc_102686, and meeting_doc_201621
